California
Fair Political
Practices Commission
}tarch 7, 199 0
Janes R. Felton
Burke, willians and Sorensen
one wilshire Building
524 South Grand Avenue, 11th Floor
Los Angel,es, CA 90017
Re: Your Request for Advice
Our FiIe No. A-90-052
Dear Mr. Felton:
This is in response to your request for advice reqarding the
,.=" ,iifi"g 
provisions of tire poli€ical Reforn Act (the rrAsgrt1.1
OUESTION
Under the terms of its franchj.se agreement with the city, the
public access television channel niI} televise the rneetings of the
iity council. Does inclusion of a photograph of -the city council,
accompanied by the nanes'gf the councilnenbers, in the program
gnride violate the uass neiling provisions of the Act?
CONCLUSION
Inctusion of a photograph of the city council, accompanied by
the names of the counci lnembers, in the program guide would
violate the nass nailing provisions of the Act. However, a roster
f.isting of the names of the councilmernbers nay be included in the
progran guide.
FACTS
The city of west Covina has entered into an agreement with
l,[t. San Antonio College and Cencorn Cable Television (Cencom).
This agreeDent has created an entity entitled MSAC cablevision
(MSAC). cencom has a nonexclusive franchise for the operati,on and
promotion of cable television within the city's boundaries. I{SAC
provides progranming on a community access channel seen over
1 cor..rr^"nt code sections 81ooo-91015. A11 statutory references
are to the covernment code unless otherwise indicated. Commission
regulations appear at 2 California Code of Regulations Section
18000, gE seq. A11 references to regulations are to Title 2,
Division 6 of the california Code of Regulations.
Sacramento CA 95804.0807 . (916)322-5660
428 J Street,Suite 800 0 P.0.Box 807 ●


File No. A-90-052
Page 2
Cencom pursuant to the franchise agreement.
40 percent of MSAC′
s yearly budget.
The city contributes
The agreement also has created a cable communications co-
operative board, a pubtic access subconmittee, and an education
access subcommittee. One member of the city staff sits on the
cable cornmunications cooperative board which acts as an advisory
board to MSAC. No members of the city staff participate in the
two subconmittees.
cencom is the only cable franchise within the city's
boundaries. Any resident of west Covina who wishes to obtain
cable television service must subscribe to cencom. AI1 solicita-
tion for new subscribers is done by cencoD. Subscribers receive
the cencom progran gpide which includes prograrnming on the publi'c
access channel.
AII of the employees of MSAC are enPloyees of the college.
These enployees are solely responsible for the printing of MsAc's
progran guide which pronotes prograrnming on the cornmunity access
i:trannef 6nfy. The city does not participate in the preparation of
MsAc's progran guide and has no control over its contents. A11 of
trtsAc's tuli mail is run throuqh a bulk rnail perrnit of the college.
usAc distributes its progran guide to all subscribers to cencom.
Under the franchise agreement, Cencom will televise the rneet-
ings of the west Covina city councj.l' on the public access channef.
l.lsic wants to advertise in its program guide that the neetings
will be televised to the residents of west Covina. The program
guide will be distributed to more than 200 subscribers. MSAC has
isked the perrnission of the city council to include a photograph
of the city council in its progran guide. This photograPh rrilI be
used for advertising purposes and to notify the residents of west
Covina that the neetings of the city council wiLl be televised.
The councilmembers' nanes would be printed along with the
phptograph.
You are requesting advice on behalf of the city of west
Covina to determine whether the use of a photograph of the city
council in the program guide would violate the nass Dailing provi-
sions of the Act. You also want to detennine whether the use of
the counci Lnembers' names aLone would be siroilarly violative of
the provisions of the Act. You are especially interested in the
applicability of our Harper Advice Letter, No. I-89-435 (copy
enclosed), to your facts. In Harper, the staff found that the
inclusion of a photograph of elected school board rnenbers in
school yearbooks would not violate the mass nailing provisions of
the Act. You believe that your facts are sufficiently analogous
to the facts in Harper, supra, to $rarrant the conclusion that
inclusion of a photograph of the city council lrith the names of
the councilmembers in the program guide would not violate the
provisions of the Act.


File No. A-90-052
Page 3
ANALYS I S
The Act defines a mass uaiJ-ing as tlro hundred or more
substantj,ally sinilar pieces of nai1. (Section 82041.5.) Section
89oo1 provides that no newsletter or other nass mailing sha11 be
sent at public expense. Read together and taken literalIy, 
these
two sections could be construed to ban a1l nailings of 20o or nore
substantially si:nilar docunents if the rnailings are done at public
expense.
The commission has enacted Regulation 189012 (copy enclosed)
to implement the provisions of section 89001. Under Regulation
18901, a tnass roaiJ.ing is prohibited if it trfeaturesrr an elected
officer ttaffiliatedrr lrith the agency which produces or sends the
nailing, or includes the name, office, photograph, or other refer-
ence to an elected officer affiliated with the agency uhich
produces or sends the nailing and the urailing is I'prepared or sent
in cooperation, consultation, coordination, or concert with the
elected officer.'r 
(RegTulation 18901(a) (2).) 
A rnass urailing is
also prohibited if any of the costs of distribution is paid for
with public moneys or costs of design, production, and printing
exceeding $5o.oo are paid with public moneys, and the design,
production, or printing is done with the intent of sending the
itern. (Regulation 18901(a) (3).) 
For purPoses of our discussion,
we assune that preparation of a photograph of the city council
would cost in excess of S50'
An elected official is 'raffiliated" with the agency which
produces or sends the rnailing if the official 
is a rnenber, of-
ficer, or enployee of the agency, or of a subunit thereof, such as
a committee, or lrho has supervisory control over the agency, or
who appoints one or more urembers of the agency. (Regrulation
18901(c) (1).) 
vie conclude that the city council is affiliated
uith MsAc because the city council has supervisory control over
MSAC through the cable cornruunication cooperative board. This
board is, in fact, an advisory board which controls the activities
of MsAc. lloreover, the city council appoints one member of this
board and contributes 40 percent of the operating budget of MSAC.
It is clear, then, that the city council is affiliated with MSAC.
However, affiliation 
alone does not nake the use of an
elected official,s 
narue in a mass rnailing prohibited. 
The second
prong of the test is whether the rnailing is prepared or sent in
.,'. The Conrnission approved the repeal and reenactment of Regula-
tion 18901 in December 1989. The regulatj,on approved by th6 Con-
nission will be subnitted to the Office ot aarninistratiie Law in
the near future. Although the new regulation is not in effect
untir reviewed by the office of Adninistrative Lae/ and filed with
the. secretary of state, the new regulation represents the current
policy of the conmission and te are advising |ou accoraingfy.


File No. A-90-052
Page 4
cooperation, consultation, coordination or concert with the
elected officer. 
where the nailing has been prepared or sent in
cooperation, consultation, coordination or concert with the
elected officer, any use of the elected offj,cer's nane, photograph
or office, or any reference to the offj,cer is prohibited.
(Regrulation 18901( (a) (2) (B) . )
If the rnailing has not been prepared or sent in cooperation,
consultation, coordination or concert with the elected officer,
use of the elected offj.cer's name is perrnitted provided the of-
ficial is not featured in the mailing. 
(Regulation
18901((a) (2) (A).) A publication rrfeaturesrr an eLected of f j.cer if
the iten mailed includes the elected officer's photograph or
signature, or singles out the elected officer by the manner of
display of his or her nane or office in the layout of the
docunent, such as by headlines, captions, type size, typeface, or
type color. 
(Regulation 18901(c) (2).)
Publication of pictures of the city council in the MsAc
program guide would rrfeaturetr the public officials. 
Because your
facts indicate that the city council is affiliated uith MsAc,
which will produce the progran guide, inclusion of a photograph of
the city council in the program guide is prohibited. 
However,
publication of a roster listing of the name and title of each
councilmernber is permissible provided that the nanes of all the
elected officers appear in the sane type sj.ze, typeface, type
color, and location on the page. (Regul.ation 18901(b) (r.).)
we reached a different conclusion in Harper, supra, because
purchase of the yearbooks is an rrunsolicitedt' request. A request
is unsolicited when it is not induced by the elected of f j,cer or
any third person acting at his or her UEtrest. (Regulation
18901(c) (4) (A) .) 
Schoot board nembers play no role in inducing
students to purchase the yearbooks. ff any solicitation 
occurs,
it is on behalf of other students involved in yearbook Production,
not on behalf of board metnbers. Thus, the yearbooks are
rrunsolicitedrt and are not 'tnass mailings'r subject to the restric-
tions of the Act.
conversely, the usAc program gruides are sent to all those who
subscribe to Cencom. Subscribers to Cencon expect to receive the
cencon program guide. Receipt of the Cencom progran guide is part
and parcel of theii contract for cable tel,evision. Regulation
L8901(c) (4) (E) provides as follows:
A person who subscribes to newspapers or other
periodicals published by persons other than elected
officers shal1 be deened to have made unsolicited
requests for naterial published in those subscrip-
tion publications.
The Commission has advised that this subdivision includes
periodicals to which a person subscribes as well as periodicals
published by Dembership organizations to $/hich the nernber belongs.


File No. A-90-052
Page 5
Thus, persons who subscribe to Cencon will be deened to have
made an unsolicited request to receive cencortr's program guide,
including a picture of the city council vhich might be published
therein. Hotever, they wiJ.l not be deemed to have nade an
unsolic.ited request to receive materials fron third parties who
are using Cencom's nailing 1ist. 
The public access program guides
distributed by lltsAc constitute such third-party nailings.
Therefore, material published in the l,{sAc gnride does not fa1I
within the exception set forth in subdivision (c) (4) (E).
Should you have any further guestions regarding this matter,
do not hesitate to call ne at (916) 322-59oL.
Sincerely,
Kathryn E. Donovan
General Counsel
